physics.js:130 Uncaught TypeError: window.collideRectRect is not a function
    at checkEntityCollision (physics.js:130:19)
    at Bat.checkPlayerCollision (entities.js:358:33)
    at Bat.update (entities.js:387:14)
    at game.js:122:38
    at Array.forEach (<anonymous>)
    at updateGame (game.js:122:23)
    at p.draw (game.js:60:17)
    at _main.default.redraw (p5.js:70809:27)
    at _draw (p5.js:63046:25)
analysis:
- Issue identified: The game crashes with `Uncaught TypeError: window.collideRectRect is not a function`. This occurs because `p5.collide2d` functions are not attached to the global `window` object when p5.js is running in instance mode (which this game uses), or the library is not loading as expected.
- Files affected: `physics.js`
- Changes needed: Replace the dependency on `window.collideRectRect` in `checkEntityCollision` with a standard AABB (Axis-Aligned Bounding Box) collision check. This removes the external dependency issue and ensures collision detection works reliably regardless of p5's initialization mode.
